description: Dark raptor floating initially floating by itself in the wind over Burlington Cemetery. Initially thought it was a Turkey Vulture and wanted to verify it as a county bird. It came low over the cemetery and I realized it had a thick white band on the underside of the tail and a small dark bill with a yellow base. I couldn't really see the other thin white bands on the tail but the legs were definitely yellow. The wings were much too narrow for Common Black-Hawk and the tail pattern was wrong. Having watched ZTHA and COBL at Tubac Bridge in AZ during migration, I felt confident this bird was a Zone-tailed Hawk!  After the initial sighting, my wife and I chased the bird around Burlington where it merged with at least a dozen Turkey Vultures, floating over the town.  I was surprised how similar it was to a Turkey Vulture until you had it in binocular view.
